source_old_req: The temperature_sensor component shall ensure that the value of its output port dataout remains within the closed interval [0, 20]. Additionally, the component shall maintain an internal variable k, initialized to 10, which updates each cycle according to the following rule: if the previous cycle's k value is greater than or equal to 20, then k shall be decremented by 1; if the previous cycle's k value is less than or equal to 0, then k shall be incremented by 1; otherwise, k shall remain unchanged. An assertion shall be included to verify that the value of the output port dataout matches the current value of the internal variable k.

expected_check.note: Existing requirement kept because it is explicit and non-vague.

quality_policy.traceable: Every item has trace_refs and expected_check metadata tied to AADL facts.
quality_policy.principle: Every generated sentence must name an explicit property assignment or structural connection; ordinary feature declarations are not used as rebuilt requirements.

behavior_style_policy.basis: front-110 AGREE-derived requirements favor implementable behavior, constants, assumptions, guarantees, routing, and bounded properties.
behavior_style_policy.rule: Do not describe unsupported validity/legal-boundary semantics unless the AADL model exposes variables, values, or properties that can implement them.

paragraph_style_policy.rule: Req.txt is written as a natural-language requirement paragraph; per-item traceability stays in requirement_items.
paragraph_style_policy.basis: Front-110 AGREE-derived requirements generally combine function, assumptions, guarantees, and implementation notes into 1-4 sentence paragraphs.
